coreIssue: This performance conversation is mainly about making the quality bar concrete for a Junior Brand Designer so feedback becomes usable rather than subjective.
openingQuestion: What do you use today to judge whether a brand design deliverable is ready to share, and where does that standard still feel fuzzy?
listenFor: whether he names a specific recent deliverable, review, or revision round instead of speaking only in generalities
whether prior feedback shows up as a changed step in his process, such as seeking examples earlier or checking work against criteria before sharing
if they describe a concrete moment when expectations felt unclear and can say what example or signal would have made the bar usable
avoid: do not turn the meeting into a stack of subjective taste notes without naming observable criteria for junior-level work
do not ask Carlos to fix quality and reliability at once; choose one immediate practice he can apply on his next design task
goodOutcome: You and Carlos have agreed what good junior-level Brand Designer work looks like on one current type of deliverable and one immediate practice he will use before sharing work.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, bring one recent piece of Carlos's work plus the feedback you gave on it, and mark two observable criteria that distinguish acceptable from strong junior-level execution.
